title: Continuous Profiling: Finding Where the CPU Actually Goes
blurb: Metrics say a service is slow; profiling says which function. How always-on sampling profilers work, how to read a flame graph, and what it takes to ingest every runtime into one model.
blurbHome: Metrics say a service is slow; profiling says which line. How always-on sampling profilers work at ~1% overhead, how to read a flame graph (interactive - click to zoom), and what it takes to ingest every runtime into one model.

title: Anomaly Detection for Alerting, at Nanosecond Cost
blurb: How to take metric anomaly detection from 200ms per evaluation to 8.5 nanoseconds and 100k+ timeseries, by moving every expensive thing out of the hot path.
blurbHome: How to take metric anomaly detection from 200ms per evaluation to 8.5 nanoseconds and 100k+ timeseries, by moving every expensive thing out of the hot path. With hand-drawn diagrams and an interactive anomaly-band you can drag.
